findings: Verified identity: the plate depicts an Eastern Kingbird (Tyrannus tyrannus) with one head, one beak, two wings, two legs, and one tail. Its dark head/upperparts, white underparts, broad-headed flycatcher form, short straight dark bill, pointed wings, and square dark tail with a white terminal band agree with the attached field references and live sources.
All visible taxonomic, measurement, habitat, behavior, field-mark, and concealed crown-patch claims are supported. No material factual or anatomical issues found.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the plate.

verification_sources.title: Eastern Kingbird Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
